---
title: "nitrix.geometry.intersection"
generated: true
signature-hash: be630a4f98371bdf
description: "Triangle-mesh self-intersection detection and removal."
listing:
  id: nitrix-geometry-intersection-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-geometry-intersection/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.geometry.intersection`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Triangle-mesh self-intersection detection and removal.

A **host-side QA / cleanup** tool, not a differentiable kernel: a uniform-grid
broad phase finds candidate face pairs, a segment-triangle (Moller-Trumbore)
narrow phase confirms genuine crossings, and [`remove_self_intersections`](nitrix-geometry-intersection/remove_self_intersections.qmd)
relaxes the offending vertices with Laplacian smoothing.

**Use as a post-hoc cleanup, never inside a jitted loop.**  The broad/narrow
phase is host-side NumPy and cannot run under `lax.fori_loop`; surface movers
like [`deform_to_sdf`](nitrix-geometry-surface/deform_to_sdf.qmd) regularise in-loop with the jittable Laplacian
fraction instead, and call this afterwards if a guard is wanted.

Limitation: the narrow phase detects transversal (edge-pierces-face) crossings
-- the self-intersections a deformed surface actually produces -- but not
exactly-coplanar overlaps (a documented, rare case).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n_pairs` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `2` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-geometry-intersection-symbols}
:::
